chunk-4b44bc493864868ddf485aeae9d36d74.content: [{"id":"c_0001","ts":"2024-01-18T23:00:00-05:00","source":"ai_chat","type":"chat_turn","text":"USER: I undercharged a client again. $600 for a brand identity that took 40 hours. How do I stop doing this? ASSISTANT: Pricing anxiety is almost always the root cause. The fix isn't a rate card — it's building the muscle of saying numbers out loud before they feel comfortable. Try this: before your next proposal call, write the number on paper and say it aloud three times. Then on the call, say it and stop talking. Silence feels like rejection but it's usually just processing. What does $2,200 mean to you — does it feel like 'too much to ask'?","tags":["freelance","pricing","confidence"],"refs":[],"pii_level":"synthetic"},{"id":"c_0002","ts":"2024-02-25T22:30:00-05:00","source":"ai_chat","type":"chat_turn","text":"USER: I have ADHD and I'm struggling to finish projects. Any strategies that actually work? ASSISTANT: A few things that work specifically for ADHD: body doubling (work alongside someone, even on video), 25-minute Pomodoros with hard stops, and 'minimum viable progress' — define the smallest possible next step rather than the full task. Finishing is a separate skill from starting. What's the project you're most stuck on right now?","tags":["adhd","productivity","freelance"],"refs":[],"pii_level":"synthetic"},{"id":"c_0003","ts":"2024-04-07T21:00:00-05:00","source":"ai_chat","type":"chat_turn","text":"USER: I want to build a following on Instagram for my design work. Where do I start? ASSISTANT: The fastest path is process content — show how you think, not just what you make. Behind-the-scenes sketches, revision rounds, before-and-after comparisons. Design Twitter/Instagram follows people who explain their decisions, not just people who have beautiful feeds. Post consistently for 60 days before evaluating. What kind of work do you want to attract more of?","tags":["social_media","design","business","portfolio"],"refs":[],"pii_level":"synthetic"},{"id":"c_0004","ts":"2024-05-30T22:00:00-05:00","source":"ai_chat","type":"chat_turn","text":"USER: I want to learn motion design. What's the fastest path given I already know Figma? ASSISTANT: After Effects is the industry standard — your Figma skills translate well since you already think in layers and frames. Start with: School of Motion free content, then Motion Design School for structured learning. Your first project should be animating your own logo — familiar subject, contained scope. Budget 3-4 months of 2 hours/week to get employable basics. Have you looked at Rive for web-based motion — it's closer to Figma's paradigm and increasingly in demand.","tags":["design","skills","learning"],"refs":[],"pii_level":"synthetic"},{"id":"c_0005","ts":"2024-07-22T23:30:00-05:00","source":"ai_chat","type":"chat_turn","text":"USER: I'm comparing myself to people my age who have full-time jobs and benefits. It's messing with my head. ASSISTANT: This is real and it's worth sitting with rather than deflecting. What specifically feels harder — the financial security, the social validation, the sense of a clear path? Comparison is usually pointing at something you actually want.
